assumptions: a game-agnostic scalar readout is invariant under an arbitrary permutation of the action axis
an index-valued readout (the committed move) is equivariant under that permutation
the dihedral D4 group of a square board acts on policies as a cell-index permutation
cross-move dispersion operators are symmetric functions of the per-move series (move-order invariant)
prohibited_inferences: a clean audit proves QUARTZ play strength
a clean audit proves the Rust engine is fully game-independent (only the audited Python readouts are covered)
a flag is a bug without first checking the operator's intended transform law
transfer to neural-MCTS equivariance without measuring the network itself
